# komodo_procedure (Resource)

Manages a Komodo procedure resource. Procedures are ordered sequences of execution stages that can be scheduled, triggered via webhook, or run manually.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "komodo_procedure" "example" {
  name = "my-procedure"

  stages = jsonencode([
    {
      name    = "Deploy Stage"
      enabled = true
      executions = [
        {
          enabled   = true
          execution = {
            type   = "DeployStack"
            params = { stack = "my-stack" }
          }
        }
      ]
    }
  ])

  schedule        = "0 2 * * *"
  schedule_format = "Cron"
  schedule_enabled = true
  failure_alert   = true
  webhook_enabled = true
}
```

## Argument Reference

- `name` (Required) - The unique name of the procedure. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `stages` (Optional) - JSON array of procedure stages. Each stage is an object with `name` (string), `enabled` (bool), and `executions` (array of `EnabledExecution` objects). Use `jsonencode()` to construct this value.
- `schedule_format` (Optional) - The schedule format. One of `Cron` or `English`.
- `schedule` (Optional) - The schedule expression (cron string or English description).
- `schedule_enabled` (Optional) - Whether the schedule is enabled.
- `schedule_timezone` (Optional) - Timezone for the schedule (IANA TZ identifier, e.g. `America/New_York`).
- `schedule_alert` (Optional) - Whether to send an alert when the procedure runs on schedule.
- `failure_alert` (Optional) - Whether to send an alert when the procedure fails.
- `webhook_enabled` (Optional) - Whether to allow triggering the procedure via webhook.
- `webhook_secret` (Optional, Sensitive) - Override the default webhook secret for this procedure.

## Attribute Reference

In addition to the above arguments, the following computed attributes are exported:

- `id` - The procedure identifier (ObjectId).

## Import

Procedures can be imported using the procedure ID:

```shell
terraform import komodo_procedure.example <procedure-id>
```
